records.ety: [F. errer, L. errare; akin to G. irren, OHG. irran, v. t., irrōn, v. i., OS. irrien, Sw. irra, Dan. irre, Goth, arzjan to lead astray, airzise astray.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Erred (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Erring (?; 277, 85).]

defs: To wander; to roam; to stray.
quotes: What seemeth to you, if there were to a man an hundred sheep and one of them hath erred.

defs: To deviate from the true course; to miss the thing aimed at.
To miss intellectual truth; to fall into error; to mistake in judgment or opinion; to be mistaken.
quotes: The man may err in his judgment of circumstances.

defs: To deviate morally from the right way; to go astray, in a figurative sense; to do wrong; to sin.
quotes: Do they not err that devise evil?
